best_extraction.authors: Thomson SJ, Tavakkolizadeh M, Love-Jones S, Patel NK, Gu JW, Bains A, Doan Q, Moffitt M
best_extraction.title: Effects of Rate on Analgesia in Kilohertz Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation: Results of the PROCO Randomized Controlled Trial
best_extraction.journal_name: Neuromodulation: Technology at the Neural Interface
best_extraction.study_question: Do 1-10 kHz stimulation rates provide equivalent analgesia, and how does charge usage differ?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 20 adults with chronic low back pain, with or without leg pain, implanted with SCS systems
best_extraction.outcome_measures: ED-NRS pain, charge per second, ODI, EQ-5D-5L, PSQI, PGIC
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Low back pain 90+ days, prior pain care, mean pain 5+, stable opioids, ODI 20-80
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: All 1-10 kHz frequencies gave similar pain relief; 1 kHz used substantially less charge.

reasoning: All models agreed strongly on core bibliographic fields. I chose the fuller journal title and issue-inclusive pages from higher-confidence consensus. Study details were merged: all agreed on 20 participants, clinical trial, 3-month follow-up, equivalent analgesia across 1-10 kHz, and lower charge at 1 kHz.

field_level_decisions.journal_name: Preferred full journal title from Anthropic and Google.
field_level_decisions.volume_issue_pages: Used 21(1):67-76; two models included issue, one omitted it.
field_level_decisions.study_question: Merged OpenRouter and Anthropic formulations for analgesia and charge.
field_level_decisions.population_assessed: Combined consensus details, keeping participant count and implanted SCS status.
field_level_decisions.follow_up_duration: Used shared final follow-up duration; omitted crossover period detail for brevity.
field_level_decisions.inclusion_criteria: Used overlapping criteria present across extractions.
